steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Use tangent properties and perimeter to find the tangent segment length
steps.content:
Tangent lines to a circle are perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency, so $\angle GMH = \angle GNH = 90^{\circ}$. Also, tangent segments from an external point to a circle are equal, so $HM = HN$.

The perimeter of quadrilateral $GMHN$ is:
$$GM + MH + HN + NG = 1,568$$
Since $GM = GN = 144$ (both radii) and $MH = HN = t$ (equal tangent segments), we have:
$$144 + t + t + 144 = 1,568$$
$$288 + 2t = 1,568$$
$$2t = 1,280$$
$$t = 640 \text{ mm}$$

steps.title: Apply Pythagorean theorem to find GH
steps.content:
In right triangle $GMH$ with the right angle at $M$, we use the Pythagorean theorem:
$$GH^2 = GM^2 + MH^2$$
$$GH^2 = 144^2 + 640^2$$
$$GH^2 = 20,736 + 409,600$$
$$GH^2 = 430,336$$
$$GH = 656 \text{ mm}$$
That matches Choice D — $656$.
